intro: The most concrete split between Bearer and Black is the pricing model: Bearer runs on freemium, while Black uses free and open-source. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Bearer carries high lock-in (oss data security scanner) and freemium; Black carries low lock-in (oss python formatter) and free and open-source. Bearer is a fit when you are mobile. Black is a fit when you are mobile. The honest trade-off: Bearer is explicitly not for hobby projects; Black is explicitly not for hobby projects. On the product side, Bearer is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Data Flow Security, and Black is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Python Formatter. Common Bearer use cases include vulnerability scanning in ci/cd. Common Black use cases include vulnerability scanning in ci/cd.
quick_take: Bearer is for mobile; Black is for mobile; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Bearer if its freemium pricing model fits your budget shape better than Black's free model, and you are mobile.
verdict_b: Choose Black if you want a fully free, open-source option and can handle self-hosting or self-support.

faq.a: Headline yes, Black is free and open-source while Bearer uses freemium. But the total cost depends on self-hosting and maintenance effort for Black versus managed convenience from Bearer. Factor in both.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Bearer to Black?
faq.a: Migration from Bearer is harder: Bearer is rated high lock-in (oss data security scanner). You can still move, but expect schema-mapping, export tooling and a longer cutover window. Budget engineering time accordingly.

On our scoring Black rates 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Bearer. That suggests Black feels more polished for everyday developer tasks. We still recommend a short hands-on trial on your stack before standardising.

faq.q: Is Black a good alternative to Bearer?
faq.a: Black targets the same best-fit audience as Bearer (mobile). Note that Black is explicitly not for hobby projects, so it only replaces Bearer if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (high vs low), which is the main thing to weigh.
